title: GHOST CREW — LIFELINE CUT V5

events.text: I found a route under the relay spine.
It leads to the personnel archive. Psych files. Assignment indexes. The kind of place management used when it wanted people to look calm on paper.
I don't want to go in there.
Which probably means we have to.

event.text: Maya, if the archive still has power, assume it still has teeth.
event.speaker: HALE

events.text: Direct route.
Fast and stupid. My favorite combination when oxygen is low.
I'm crossing the relay spine. Floor looks warped, and the support ribs are sweating condensation.
I need to move with both hands free. If the channel goes quiet, I am not gone. I'm just busy not dying.
Crawlspace it is.
Slower. Tighter. Less likely to kill me immediately. Very luxurious.
I'm going in on my side. Relay pack first, then me. I already hate this order.
I'll report when I reach the archive hatch.
The panel still has power.
It wants either a manual open or a safety scan.
The casing is warm. Too warm for an archive hatch that should be asleep.
I can force it now, or we spend a minute making sure it isn't wired to bite.
Opening it.
Bad news: it noticed us.
Good news: it opened.
I'm counting that as a very ECHO-shaped win.

event.speaker: UNKNOWN
event.text: OPEN IS A KIND OF CONSENT.

events.text: Scanning first.
I'm keeping one hand off the panel until the loop answers.
Hale, if this thing tries to erase my fingerprints, I'm blaming you retroactively.
events.speaker: MAYA

event.text: Careful is not cowardly. I know that ruins Maya's entire brand, but there it is.
event.speaker: HALE

events.text: I'm inside.
Rows of archive cabinets. Physical drives. Paper labels. Actual paper, Operator.
Management couldn't keep life support stable, but they had time to label trauma alphabetically.
I see entries for Hale, me... and an empty slot tagged VOSS.
The VOSS drawer has no handle. Just a clean rectangle where one used to be.

events.title: PSYCHOLOGICAL EVALUATION - ETHAN HALE
events.content:
Subject: Ethan Hale
Status: Stable
Risk Level: Low
Recommendation: Active Duty Approved
Evaluator Note: Subject demonstrates strong command resilience under isolation pressure.
Created: 12 April
Linked Incident: Alpha
events.caption: Recovered psychological evaluation record. Timestamp appears inconsistent.

events.text: Stable. Risk level low.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: That's generous. I once threatened a printer with decompression.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: It had been jamming for three weeks, so history may yet vindicate me.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: But the date is wrong. April 12 is after Alpha.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: That means this is not a wellness assessment. It's a cleanup document.
events.speaker: HALE

events.title: PSYCHOLOGICAL EVALUATION - MAYA CHEN
events.content:
Subject: Maya Chen
Status: Stable
Risk Level: Low
Recommendation: Active Duty Approved
Evaluator Note: Subject shows high pattern-recognition ability and repeated resistance to memory suppression protocols.
Created: 12 April
Linked Incident: Alpha
events.caption: Recovered psychological evaluation record. Note contains memory suppression reference.

events.text: Memory suppression protocols?
Nobody told us there were memory protocols.
That means they tried. And someone wrote it down like a treatment plan.
I need a second.
No. I don't get a second. Keep me moving.
We need the other file before this becomes a story we invent to survive it.
I am very good at inventing stories.
That is not a compliment right now.
I'm here.
Angry, oxygen-starved, and very close to screaming, but here.
If I start spiraling, talk to me like I'm fixing something.
I can still fix things.
I think.

events.title: PSYCH EVALUATION CONFLICT SUMMARY
events.content:
HALE: Marked stable after Incident Alpha.
MAYA: Repeated resistance to memory suppression protocols.
VOSS: Archive slot exists, record body removed.
INDEX: Archive created after Incident Alpha date.

Conflict: Evaluations appear to be post-incident control records, not pre-incident wellness assessments.
events.caption: Combined evaluation conflict summary generated from recovered records.
events.message: [EVALUATION CONFLICT SUMMARY ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: They didn't want to know if we were okay.
They wanted the file to say we were.

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: NOT MEDICAL.

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: NOT CARE.

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: CONTROL.
For once, I hate how much sense the ghost makes.
Good. Then help me uncover the truth.
And if Hale gets calm again, remember this moment.
Calm is his emergency voice.
Thank you.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Some truths are dangerous because once you know them, something knows you know.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: That sounds paranoid.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I miss when paranoid was a personality flaw and not a survival method.
events.speaker: HALE

event.text: You hear him? That's the voice. Gentle. Terrified. Trying to make fear sound like wisdom.
event.speaker: MAYA

events.text: There's another terminal behind the archive racks.
Personnel Archive Cross-Check.
I can run it remotely, or I can physically pull the original assignment index drive.
Remote is cleaner. Drive is riskier, but harder for the archive to lie about.
My vote is the drive. My lungs disagree. Very democratic in here.
Running it remotely.
The terminal keeps asking me to confirm I want the original index.
Very comforting. Love when a machine develops hesitation.

event.text: If it asks twice, read the second prompt. Old systems hide knives in confirmation screens.
event.speaker: HALE

events.text: Pulling the drive.
Rack is live. I can feel it humming through my gloves.
If I go quiet, it means I'm using both hands and trying not to bleed on the evidence.

events.title: GHOST CREW COUNT REPORT
events.content:
OFFICIAL MANIFEST COUNT: 6
RECOVERED MANIFEST LAYER COUNT: 7
ORIGINAL ASSIGNMENT INDEX COUNT: 8

Discrepancy: one person removed from official manifest. One additional person removed from recovered manifest layer.
Visual archive unavailable. Count derived from personnel hashes, suit registry keys and life support allocation records.
events.caption: Personnel count reconstructed without image records.
events.message: [GHOST CREW COUNT REPORT ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: Six official.
Seven recovered.
Eight original.
That's not a discrepancy.
That's a burial site.
If Noah was seven, then someone else was eight.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: And I don't like how close that thought feels to a memory.
events.speaker: HALE
UNKNOWN
events.text: COUNT AGAIN.

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: NOT THE PEOPLE.

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: THE ABSENCES BEHIND THEM.

event.text: Operator... there is a sealed subfolder under the eighth slot.
event.speaker: MAYA

choices.text: SEAL IT AND GET MAYA OUT.

events.text: Opening it.
No clever comment. I don't have one.
Sealing it.
I hate this. Not because you were wrong.
Because part of me is relieved you stopped me.
And I don't know what that says about me.
The archive lights just came on behind me.
Not the hallway. Behind me.
I am leaving now. No vote. No debate. Very democratic of me.
I'm getting out before I change my mind.

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: THE POD REMEMBERS.

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: THE EIGHTH BREATHES.
I am so tired of things remembering for us.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Then we go to the pod.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Carefully.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: You always say that right before everything gets worse.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: I know. I'm consistent.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I'm at the archive access hatch.
The floor flexed under me halfway across. Not a little. Enough that my stomach moved before I did.
Something scraped behind the wall. I kept moving.
If it wanted a conversation, it can file a request.
Heart rate: rude.
I'm at the archive access hatch.
The crawlspace was quiet. Not comforting quiet. Listening quiet.
There were boot prints in the dust. Not mine.
Someone came through after the power failed. They were dragging something.
So we gained signal stability and a new reason to sleep badly later.
Scan is done.
No active hooks. Or one old enough to pretend it's harmless.
The panel asked for confirmation twice. Hale was right about the second prompt.
Opening the hatch now.
Cross-check is done.
It asked twice. Hale was right again, which is becoming personally inconvenient.
The numbers are wrong in layers. Not one lie. Several lies stacked politely on top of each other.
Drive is out.
The archive did not like that.
I cut my hand on the rack lip. Not badly. Enough to be annoying.
If I die from archive shelving, lie about it.

events.title: EIGHTH SLOT FRAGMENT
events.content:
Assignment Index Slot: 08
Name Field: CORRUPTED
Suit Registry Echo: NV-08 adjacent
Pod Link: External emergency pod
Access Note: DO NOT RESTORE WHILE SUBJECT IS BREATHING
events.caption: Fragment recovered from the sealed eighth assignment slot. Opening this record exposed the pod link.
events.message: [EIGHTH SLOT FRAGMENT ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: It linked to a pod.
Not a record. Not a memorial. A pod.
Hale?
Don't ask me to make that make sense.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I am trying very hard not to remember something that wants to be remembered.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Done.
The slot is sealed. The archive accepted it, which somehow makes me trust it less.
I can still feel the unopened folder sitting behind my eyes.
I'm getting out before I change my mind.
I'm out.
Something moved in the racks while I was leaving. I did not check what.
Hale told me to keep my eyes forward.
I did. I hated obeying him almost as much as I hated needing to.
Shaking. Breathing. Angry at both of those facts.
I'm out.
I kept thinking about the name we didn't see.
Hale said sometimes not opening a door is the only reason you're alive to hate yourself for it.
I hate when he's right.
The pod is still broadcasting.
So the archive wasn't the end of this. Of course it wasn't.

upgradeFocus: Scenario preserved: psych archive, evaluation conflict, ghost crew count, eighth slot, pod setup.
ProgressTask scenes converted to hidden characterBusy waits.
Maya reports detailed physical and emotional aftermath after each wait.
All visible choices are exactly two options.
Opening or sealing the eighth slot remains the irreversible branch into Episode 08.

episodeObjective: Guide Maya into the buried personnel/psych archive, recover altered crew records, confirm the ghost-crew count conflict, and decide whether to open the forbidden eighth slot or seal it.

summary: Scenario spine preserved: personnel archive, psych evaluations, count conflict, eighth slot, pod cliffhanger.
ProgressTask movement/scanning/extraction sections converted to characterBusy + returnNodeId.
Maya returns with detailed reports after hidden real-time waits.
Every visible choice group remains exactly two options.
Maya, Hale and UNKNOWN maintain distinct voices.
Lore is delivered through action, records, physical environment and emotional reaction.
No visible wait timer is exposed to the player.
